data.content:
Expanded Playwright MCP auto-approval proof, 2026-04-25

Versions rechecked locally before the run:
- Codex: codex-cli 0.125.0
- OpenCode: 1.14.24
- Playwright MCP package: @playwright/mcp 0.0.70

Harness correction:
- The first expanded Node harness attempt timed out because the wrapper left child stdin open. Codex printed/blocked on additional stdin in a smoke probe. The corrected harness closes child stdin immediately after spawn.

Expanded live-page coverage:
- The harness started a local HTTP page with unique values title=b7c0-exhaustive-title-1777117251288 and token=b7c0-token-1777117251288.
- The page exposed a button, labelled input, console log, network request, and window.__approvalProof() state reader.
- Required Playwright MCP capabilities: browser_navigate, browser_snapshot, browser_click twice, browser_type or browser_fill_form, browser_press_key, browser_evaluate or browser_run_code, browser_console_messages, browser_network_requests.
- Failure conditions: nonzero exit, timeout, approval/rejection markers, missing required tool groups, missing final proof marker, or missing proof state.

Codex command shape:
- codex exec --json -C /Users/cartine/foolery
- -c approval_policy="never"
- -c sandbox_mode="danger-full-access"
- -c mcp_servers.playwright.command="npx"
- -c mcp_servers.playwright.args=["-y","@playwright/mcp@0.0.70","--isolated","--headless"]
- -c mcp_servers.playwright.default_tools_approval_mode="approve"

OpenCode command shape:
- OPENCODE_CONFIG_CONTENT={"mcp":{"playwright":{"type":"local","command":["npx","-y","@playwright/mcp@0.0.70","--isolated","--headless"],"enabled":true}},"permission":{"playwright_*":"allow"}}
- opencode run --format json --dir /Users/cartine/foolery <prompt>

Proof excerpts:
- Codex emitted completed mcp_tool_call events for browser_navigate, browser_snapshot, browser_click x2, browser_type, browser_press_key, browser_evaluate, browser_console_messages, and browser_network_requests.
- Codex browser_evaluate returned title=b7c0-exhaustive-title-1777117251288, clicks=2, token=b7c0-token-1777117251288, enter=true, countText=2, statusText=entered:b7c0-token-1777117251288.
- OpenCode emitted completed tool_use events for playwright_browser_navigate, playwright_browser_snapshot, playwright_browser_click x2, playwright_browser_type, playwright_browser_press_key, playwright_browser_evaluate, playwright_browser_console_messages, and playwright_browser_network_requests.
- OpenCode browser_evaluate returned the same title/token/click/enter proof state.
- Final harness line: PASS exhaustive playwright approval harness title=b7c0-exhaustive-title-1777117251288 token=b7c0-token-1777117251288

Conclusion:
- The expanded proof now covers eight representative Playwright MCP capability groups across both CLIs, including browser_evaluate, the exact approval request type seen in the f354 incident.
- Codex auto-approval remains proven for the tested stdio Playwright MCP server when default_tools_approval_mode="approve" is configured.
- OpenCode auto-approval remains proven for generated Playwright MCP permission names when permission wildcard "playwright_*":"allow" is configured.
- This is stronger than the original single browser_navigate proof, but it still is not a literal execution of every Playwright MCP tool listed in @playwright/mcp 0.0.70.
